Assets Allocation in February: Overweight CSI500, Optimistic on Gold
2 月资产配置月报: 超配中证，黄金乐观
姜 婧 Jiang Jing 从业资格号 Qualification No.: F3018552 投资咨询号 Consulting No.: Z0013315
王含章 Wang Hanzhang 从业资格号 Qualification No.: F03121254 投资咨询号 Consulting No.: Z0022985

摘要 Abstract
The core market theme for February revolves around a decline in volatility following the
previous period of crowded trading. Our allocation strategy shifts toward seizing structural
opportunities as the market stabilizes.
• Equities: Overweight CSI 500 in long positions, targeting mid-cap growth.
• Precious Metals: Maintain a benchmark allocation (neutral weighting) for gold as a core
holding, while remaining on the sidelines regarding silver.
• Non-ferrous Metals: While sector fundamentals remain supportive, we expect a short-term
unwinding of crowded trades. We advise patience, waiting for market volatility to subside before
increasing exposure.
Risks: Geopolitical risks; Unexpected strengthening of the US economy, which could undermine
the logic of interest rate cuts; Chinese domestic incremental policies and economic recovery falling
short of expectations; Unforeseen tightening of liquidity; Unexpected rebound of the US dollar index.
2 月市场主线在于经过前期的拥挤交易之后，等待市场的降波过程。建议 2 月在配置思路上更加
注重把握结构性机会。权益考虑多头超配中证 500，贵金属中的黄金维持多头标配、白银观望，有色
金属在产业逻辑支撑下仍然向好，但短期内市场挤压拥挤交易泡沫，需等待市场波动率降低。
风险因素：1）地缘风险；2）美国经济意外走强，导致降息逻辑破坏；3）国内增量政策和经济
修复不及预期；4）流动性超预期收紧；5）美元指数超预期反弹。
重要提示：本报告非期货交易咨询业务项下服务，其中的观点和信息仅作参考之用，不构成对任何人投资建议。我司不会因为关注、收到或阅读本报告内容而视

only and do not constitute investment advice to anyone. CITIC Futures will not consider relevant personnel as customers due to their attention ,
receipt, or reading of this report.

Overview
In January, the market's focus remained on expectation trading. The prices of non-ferrous
metals and precious metals rose sharply. Meanwhile, the impact of geopolitical factors on some
assets was more significant, driving crude oil to rebound beyond expectations. After Powell was
appointed as the chairperson of the Federal Reserve, he corrected market expectations, causing
market volatility to rise significantly. It is expected that macro disturbances will tend to decrease in
February, and China will also face the Spring Festival holiday. It is recommended to wait for the
market to calm down.
At the global level, the overall economic situation has stabilized, and the general
direction of interest rate cuts remains unchanged. However, market focus has further shifted
towards data dependency. The ease of inflation and the weak but stable employment situation
support the increase in interest rate cuts. The nomination of Powell as the new chair of the Federal
Reserve and his policy orientation have yet to be implemented. Fluctuations in policy independence
and liquidity expectations may become the core variables for pricing in the short term. Meanwhile,
the risk of a U.S. government shutdown and geopolitical disturbances still exist. Attention should be
paid to the asset price disturbances caused by expectation differences and event shocks.
On China’s front, the economic operation has been generally stable, and policy
expectations have continued to rise. In the first year of the "15th Five-Year Plan", the demand for
a "good start" has intensified. Incremental measures such as the issuance and interest subsidy,
guarantee, etc. of broad fiscal tools are expected to be implemented in the first quarter in advance.
The monetary side continues to follow the "quantitative expansion and price stability, precise and
targeted injection" strategy, and there are still operational windows for structural interest rate cuts
and liquidity injection. Against the backdrop of a relatively slow recovery pace and the need for
verification of the improvement in the real estate and traditional investment, policy expectations and
liquidity improvement may continue to dominate market pricing. In February, domestic risky assets
may still be in the stage driven by expectations.
In terms of major assets, we place greater emphasis on the structural opportunities in
the allocation. The policy expectations and additional liquidity in the domestic equity market can
still provide upward support. The overall government bonds are neutral, with better short-term
opportunities than long-term ones, but the odds are limited. Gold in precious metals remains a long-
term holding, while silver is on hold for the time being. The non-ferrous metals remain positive under
the support of industrial logic, but in the short term, the market is crowded with trading bubbles and
needs to wait for the market volatility to decrease. The overall ferrous metals are relatively volatile,
and crude oil may rise under the support of geopolitics, but the uncertainty is high, so it is
recommended to remain vigilant.
3

Asset allocation recommendations for February:
Chinese domestic stock indices: Overweight mid-cap style in long positions. Focus on:
CSI 500.
Government bonds: Overall neutral. Long positions should include short-term bonds.
Focus on: TS.
Commodities: On hold. For non-ferrous metals, we need to pay attention to the short-term
volatility risks. The market sentiment may further decline, so we should look for opportunities for
buy-on-dip and hold for a long position after the volatility subsides. For commodities such as ferrous
metals and crude oil, they may experience wide fluctuations. We should maintain a wait-and-see
attitude.
Precious metals: Bullish on gold. Wait for volatility to wane and buy the dip. Focus on: SHFE
Gold.
1. January Review of Major Assets
In January, the main trading trend of major global assets continued the previous logic. Based
on the continuation of transactions relying on the credit of the US dollar, risky assets generally
showed a significant upward trend, but the trading congestion reached a high level and a notable
correction occurred at the end of the month.
In the equity market, the overall performance of the A-share market was significantly superior.
Major indices generally recorded positive returns, and the mid-cap and small-cap styles were
relatively stronger, reflecting that funds were more inclined to allocate to assets with high flexibility
under the background of improved policy expectations and risk preferences. The performance of the
Hong Kong stock market was relatively weaker, and it was more significantly affected by external
uncertainties and risk preferences. In terms of overseas equities, developed markets were generally
in a state of oscillation and differentiation, with the US stock market having limited upward
momentum due to valuation and interest rate fluctuations, while markets in Europe and Japan were
relatively more stable; emerging markets performed relatively better driven by the recovery of risk
preferences and the capital inflows.
In the bond market, the overall interest rate assets are weak, and the term structure is highly
differentiated. The short-term prices of domestic bond markets are relatively stable, while the
medium and long-term prices are under greater pressure. The yield curve shows a relatively steep
feature, reflecting the combined effect of inflation and policy expectations disturbances, as well as
changes in supply rhythm and duration preference. The term premium has risen due to the joint
influence of these factors. Similarly, overseas US bonds also present a similar pattern. The long-
term volatility is greater, and the market's sensitivity to fiscal, supply, and inflation paths has
increased, leading to the continuous exposure of yield curve risks.
In the foreign exchange market, the US dollar experienced a temporary correction, while non-
US currencies rose against the US dollar. The pressure on the RMB exchange rate to appreciate
4

has eased somewhat, and the euro, the pound, and the yen have generally risen along with the
decline of the US dollar.
In the commodity market, all types of commodities were generally strong. Precious metals
and related non-ferrous metal varieties performed particularly well, reflecting the concentration of
risk-averse demand and flexible capital allocation. However, the trading congestion was too high,
and a significant correction occurred at the end of the month. Copper and other non-ferrous metals
were relatively dominant due to the support of macro expectations and supply-side constraints. The
energy sector rebounded significantly supported by geopolitical and supply disruptions. The ferrous
metals sector was mainly characterized by fluctuations, alternating between weak reality and
periodic expectation trading. Some agricultural product varieties rebounded, but pork remained
under pressure.

2. Macro Environment Outlook
2.1 Global Macro
The global PMI edged down slightly to 50.4 in December, down 0.1 percentage points
month-on-month, but remained in expansionary territory.
From November to December, U.S. macro data generally showed weakening inflation, rising
unemployment, and steady consumption figures. As the data were largely lackluster, market
attention shifted more toward expectations surrounding the nomination of the new Federal Reserve
Chair.
Chart 2: US Economic Data
Sources: iFinD, CITIC Futures
On the employment front, labor market trends continued to weaken with increased
volatility. According to Wind data, U.S. nonfarm payrolls added just 50,000 jobs in December, a
notably low level. This marked a further slowdown compared with the roughly 56,000 jobs added in
November, while October had seen a sharp turn to negative growth—underscoring the lack of
continuity in labor market recovery. Although December’s job gains slightly exceeded expectations,
they were concentrated mainly on education and healthcare, as well as construction sectors linked
to artificial intelligence; other industries generally remained sluggish. On inflation, core CPI stayed
flat at low levels, further easing inflationary constraints. On the demand side, according to
Wind data, U.S. retail sales rose 0.6% month-on-month in November, with the core measure
up 0.4%, signaling a stable start to the holiday season and sustained underlying consumer
momentum.
Regarding monetary policy, the Fed announced after its January FOMC meeting that it
would keep its target interest rate unchanged in the 350–375 basis point range, in line with
expectations. Consequently, speculation over the nomination of the new Fed Chair remains
6

CITIC Futures International Research 中信期货国际化研究
a key focus for markets in the near term. With the nominee still undecided, concerns over central
bank independence now outweigh the impact of any potential delay in rate cuts.
Approaching the end of January, prediction platform Polymarket shows that the
probability of a U.S. government shutdown by late January has surged from less than 10% at
the start of the month to around 80%. To avoid a shutdown, Congress must pass 12 appropriations
bills by January 31. As of January 25, only six bills had made progress in both chambers, while the
remaining six—covering critical areas such as homeland security and defense—remained stalled.
The Senate Republicans hold just 53 seats, short of the 60 votes required to advance legislation.
According to Xinhua reports, U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) officers shot and
killed an American citizen during enforcement operations in Minnesota—the second such incident in
a month—sparking strong backlash from U.S. society and Democrats. Per Wind News, Senate
Democratic Leader Chuck Schumer explicitly stated that he would block passage of the Department
of Homeland Security budget unless provisions limiting ICE’s excessive enforcement actions were
included in the funding bill. Republicans, however, insisted on maintaining a hardline immigration
stance, refusing to split the bill or attach restrictive clauses, leaving negotiations deadlocked. On
January 31st, according to CBS News, the US Congress failed to pass part of the budget, raising
the risk of a partial government shutdown. If the funds are not available by the deadline, multiple
agencies will be forced to shut down. Departments such as the Department of Homeland Security
may be affected, while the two chambers of Congress are still deadlocked over budget differences.
We believe geopolitical factors will remain significant market disruptors in February. The
U.S. and Europe may enter detailed negotiations on the Greenland issue. While tariff threats have
been temporarily lifted, competition over Arctic resource allocation between the two sides is likely to
persist. According to CCTV News, the USS Abraham Lincoln carrier strike group has arrived in the
Middle East, meaning tensions involving Iran, the Persian Gulf, or even the Arabian Sea could see
sudden shifts in the near term. Germany will host the Munich Security Conference in February, where
developments in the Russia-Ukraine conflict and trilateral talks among the U.S., Russia, and Ukraine
should be closely watched. Domestically in the U.S., given that 2026 is a midterm election year,
neither party wants to shoulder the political cost of prolonged government paralysis. We expect that
around the January 31 deadline, the two sides may agree to some form of short-term continuing
resolution or experience a very brief “technical shutdown” before quickly reaching a compromise.
The non-US developed markets remained stable with some marginal differences.
According to Bloomberg and iFinD, affected by the weak performance of Germany, the
manufacturing PMI of the Eurozone in January was recorded at 49.4.
The overall economic sentiment of emerging markets in December was generally positive
but slightly slowed down. Although the manufacturing PMI of Vietnam and India declined in
December, they remained above the critical line and had sufficient growth momentum. In terms of
exports, the export orders of Vietnam and India showed certain resilience, while those of Malaysia,
Thailand and the Philippines declined year-on-year.

2.2 China Macro
We maintain our judgment that the domestic macroeconomic outlook for the entire quarter
remains positive. The overall investment environment for risky assets remains favorable. We
recommend that investors continue to focus on two main lines in the domestic macroeconomic
context in February: First, the expectations for the "15th Five-Year Plan" opening-year
policies. Second, the expectations for inflation to rise.

3. Outlook for Major Assets
3.1 Stock Index
Looking ahead to February, we expect the policy expectations for a strong start to the
15th Five-Year Plan and the trend of rising inflation to become the main macro narrative in
the equity market. The domestic equity market is likely to continue to remain in a trend of
strong oscillations, and we still recommend overweighting IC. As we stated in the chapter on
domestic fiscal and monetary policies in this report, in January there were significant marginal
changes in both fiscal and monetary policies. These policy changes all pointed to intensifying efforts
to achieve a strong start to the economy in the first year of the "15th Five-Year Plan". Therefore, we
expect that fiscal policies and broad fiscal expenditures will continue to exert strong leadership early
on, and the marginal easing of monetary policy may also occur at a point earlier in the year or even
in the first quarter. Thus, we believe that the first quarter may be a window with stronger expectations
for domestic policies. In addition, the expectation of a rebound in the inflation center also has a strong
positive impact on the equity market. The improvement in prices is expected to boost corporate
profits. In a warming policy environment, coupled with the expected recovery of corporate profits, we
still recommend that investors continue to overweight the stock index after the adjustment.

3.2 Commodities
3.2.1 Precious Metals
Looking ahead to February, the market will enter a period of verifying the Fed's policy
path and a window for geopolitical trading. Under the dual benefits of credit hedging and
shortage of spot supply, it is expected to maintain a volatile upward trend. From a macro
perspective, the focus is on the risk-off sentiment brought about by the Warsh trade, as well as the
development of geopolitical events such as those in Iran and Ukraine, along with the prevailing risk-
averse sentiment. From a fundamental standpoint, the main attention is on the physical demand
after the Chinese Spring Festival and the gold purchasing behavior of the central bank. The situation
where the credit reconstruction of the US dollar and the strong physical demand for gold resonates
has not undergone a fundamental reversal. In the short term, the prices of gold and silver are likely
to remain in a state of significant volatility. It is recommended to take advantage of the decline in the
market and the decrease in volatility to establish positions at a low price.

3.2.2 Non-Ferrous Metals
The boom in the non-ferrous metals market is the result of the convergence of macroeconomic
factors and tight supply. Looking ahead to February, the volatility of market prices is likely to increase
through the interaction between reality and expectations. Therefore, in terms of strategies, we
suggest that in the non-ferrous metals sector, a structural bottom-up allocation approach
should be adopted, with a focus on the most supply-constrained products such as copper,
aluminum, and tin. We should also be cautious of the correction risks of minor metal products that
have experienced excessive gains and lack fundamental support. Pay attention to the accumulation
speed of domestic social inventories after the Spring Festival and the policy guidance of the new
chairperson of the Federal Reserve.

3.2.3 Ferrous Metals
The fluctuations in the ferrous commodity market at the beginning of the year are essentially the
result of the collision between macro expectations, the spillover of commodity market sentiment, and
the weak reality. It is expected that the ferrous commodity sector will maintain wide fluctuations, and
attention should be paid to the changes in inventory after the Spring Festival.

Looking ahead to February, the oil price remains a game of balancing the medium-term
oversupply expectations with the short-term geopolitical escalation and supply disruptions.
It is expected to remain volatile. The macro environment is dominated by geopolitics, and whether
the situations in Venezuela and Iran will escalate still needs to be observed. From the fundamental’s
perspective, the progress of Kazakhstan's supply recovery and the disturbance caused by extreme
weather; in the medium term, there is a lag effect in the adjustment of US shale oil production, and
the production center is unlikely to undergo a substantive reversal. With the support of geopolitical
risk speculation and supply disruptions, the downside space for the oil price is limited.

3.3 Bonds
In February, we expect that the short-term performance may still be better than the
medium and long-term. The overall risk-reward ratio is moderate. The logic mainly lies in two
long-term trends: ①The PPI may gradually recover in 2026, which means that the trend of real
interest rates will generally decline, weakening the necessity for rate cuts; ②the continuous
implementation of "anti-involution competition" policies will also boost the expectation of price
recovery and lower real interest rates. Additionally, as fiscal efforts still tend to be proactive, the
supply of bonds in the short term (first quarter) is expected to be relatively strong, which may also
limit the space for interest rate decline.

4. Asset Allocation Recommendations
In February, we placed greater emphasis on defensive strategies during the market's
downward correction process, waiting for the correction to be completed before making new
investments. The policy expectations and additional liquidity in the domestic equity market can still
provide upward support. The overall government bonds are neutral, with better opportunities in the
short term than in the long term, but the odds are limited. Gold in precious metals remains a long-
term bet, and the trend of non-ferrous metals is not changed under the support of macro and supply
logic, but short-term fluctuations need to be cautious. ferrous metals are generally volatile, and crude
oil may rise under the support of geopolitical factors, but the uncertainty is high, so it is recommended
to remain vigilant.
February Asset Allocation Recommendations:
Chinese domestic Equity Indices: Long position heavily allocated to mid-cap style. Focus
on: CSI 500.
Government Bonds: Overall neutral. Long positions should include short-term bonds.
Focus on: TS.
Commodities: On hold. For non-ferrous metals, pay attention to the short-term volatility risks.
The market sentiment may further decline and look for opportunities to buy low and hold for a long
position after the volatility subsides. For ferrous metals and crude oil, they may experience wide
fluctuations. Maintain a wait-and-see attitude.
Precious metals: Hold a long position as a standard. Pay attention to the stabilization of
volatility and the opportunity to re-enter the market after the correction. Focus: SHFE Gold
Risks: Geopolitical risks; Unexpected strengthening of the U.S. economy; Chinese domestic
incremental policies and economic recovery falling short of expectations; Liquidity tightening beyond
expectations; U.S. Dollar Index rebounding more than expected.

1 月市场主线仍然聚焦预期交易，有色和贵金属大涨，同时，地缘因素扰动对部分资产提振更加
显著，驱动原油超预期反弹。沃什提任美联储主席之后纠偏市场预期，市场波动大幅上升，预计 2 月
宏观扰动将趋于减小，国内也将面临春节长假，建议等待市场降波。
海外层面，全球经济整体企稳，降息大方向未改，但市场关注重点进一步转向数据依赖。通胀缓
和、就业偏弱企稳支撑暂停降息，沃什获得美联储新主席提名及其政策取向尚未落地，政策独立性与
流动性预期的波动可能成为阶段性定价核心变量。同时，美国政府关门风险与地缘扰动仍存，需关注
由预期差与事件冲击带来的资产价格扰动。
国内层面，经济运行总体平稳，政策预期持续升温。“十五五”开局之年“开门红”诉求强化，财政端
广义财政工具发行及贴息、担保等增量举措有望在一季度前置发力；货币端延续“量宽价稳、精准滴灌”
思路，结构性降息与流动性投放仍存在操作窗口。在现实修复节奏偏缓、地产与传统投资改善仍待验
证的背景下，政策预期与流动性改善或继续主导市场定价，2 月国内风险资产或仍处于由预期驱动的
阶段。
大类资产层面，我们更加强调配置的结构性机会。国内权益市场的政策预期和额外流动性依然可
以提供上行的支撑。国债整体中性，短端机会好于长端，但赔率有限。贵金属中的黄金维持多头标配，
白银暂时观望，有色金属在产业逻辑支撑下仍然向好，但短期内市场挤压拥挤交易泡沫，需等待市场
波动率降低。黑色商品整体偏震荡，原油或在地缘支撑下有所上行，但不确定性较大因此建议保持观
望。
2 月资产配置多空建议：
国内股指：多头超配中盘风格。关注：中证 500。
国债：整体中性，多头标配短端债券。关注：TS。
大宗商品：整体观望。有色金属关注短期波动风险，市场情绪可能进一步回调，关注降波后低吸
做多的机会。黑色、原油等商品或宽幅震荡，保持观望。
贵金属：多头标配黄金，关注波动率趋稳和回调后的补仓机会。关注：沪金。

一、1 月大类资产回顾：预期与流动性主导，结构性分化加剧
1 月全球大类资产的交易主线延续之前的逻辑，基于美元信用的交易延续，风险资产普遍呈现大
幅上涨的格局，交易拥挤度过高月末出现显著回调。
权益市场，A 股整体表现明显占优，主要指数普遍录得正收益，且中盘、中小盘风格相对更强，
反映资金在政策预期与风险偏好改善背景下更倾向于配置高弹性资产。港股表现相对偏弱，整体受外
部不确定性与风险偏好扰动影响更为明显。海外权益方面，发达市场总体偏震荡分化，美股在估值与
利率波动约束下上行动能有限，欧日等市场相对更稳；新兴市场则在风险偏好回暖与资金回流带动下
表现相对更好。
债券市场，利率资产整体偏弱且期限分化突出。国内债市短端价格相对稳健，中长端价格承压更
为明显，收益曲线呈现偏陡特征，反映在通胀与政策预期扰动、供给节奏与久期偏好变化的共同作用
下，期限溢价有所抬升。海外美债亦呈现类似格局，长端波动更大，市场对财政、供给与通胀路径的
敏感度上升，推动收益率曲线风险持续暴露。
外汇市场，美元阶段性回调，非美货币对美元上行。人民币汇率升值压力有所缓解，欧元、英镑、
日元随着美元回落而整体上行。
大宗商品市场，各品种商品整体偏强。贵金属与有色相关品种表现突出，体现避险需求与高弹性
资金配置的集中，但交易拥挤度过高月末出现显著回调；有色金属在宏观预期改善与供给端约束的支
撑下相对占优。能源板块在地缘和供应扰动支撑下反弹显著。黑色商品整体以震荡为主，在弱现实与
阶段性预期交易之间反复切换。农产品部分品种出现反弹，生猪仍然承压。

（一）海外宏观：沃什提任，联储独立性交易纠偏
全球 PMI 在 12 月略有回落，录得 50.4，环比下行 0.1 个百分点，但仍然于扩张区间。
11-12 月美国宏观数据整体呈现为通胀走弱，失业率上行，消费数据平稳。由于数据整体较为平淡，
市场的关注焦点更多集中在美联储新主席提名的预期上。

和医疗保健、及与人工智能相关的建筑领域，其他行业普遍乏力。通胀方面，核心 CPI 低位走平，通胀

货币政策方面，根据美联储的公告，1 月 FOMC 议息会议维持利率目标 350-375BP 不变，符合
预期。因此，美联储新任主席提名依然是近期市场的关注点。当前美联储新主席人选悬而未决，独立
性风险超过降息推迟的影响。
临近 1 月底，预测平台 Polymarket 显示，市场押注 1 月底美国政府发生停摆的概率已从月初的
不到 10%飙升至 80%附近。为避免关门，国会需在 1 月 31 日前通过 12 个支出法案。截至 1 月 25 日，
仅有 6 个法案在两院取得进展，剩余涉及国土安全、国防等关键领域的 6 个法案仍受阻。参议院共和党仅
有 53 席，无法达到通过法案所需的 60 票。据新华社报道，美国移民与海关执法局（ICE）在明尼苏达州
执法过程中射杀一名美国公民，为一个月内的第二起类似事件，引发美国社会和民主党强烈反弹。据 Wind
新闻的消息，参议院民主党领袖舒默明确表示，除非在拨款法案中加入限制 ICE 过度执法的条款，否则将
拒绝通过国土安全部的预算。共和党则坚持强硬移民政策，拒绝拆分法案或附加限制条款，导致谈判陷入
死局。1 月 31 日，根据 CBS 新闻报道，美国国会未能通过部分预算，导致联邦政府部分关门风险升高，
若资金在期限前不到位，多个机构将被迫停摆。国土安全部等部门可能受影响，而国会两院仍在就预算分
歧僵持不下。
我们认为地缘因素仍然将会是 2 月市场中重要的扰动因素。美欧双方或将就格陵兰岛问题进入协
议细节谈判阶段。虽然关税威胁已暂时解除，但美欧在北极资源分配上的博弈或仍将持续。根据央视新闻，
“林肯”号航母打击群已经抵达中东，因此在近期伊朗、波斯湾甚至是阿拉伯海的局势都可能出现变数。
2 月德国将举办慕尼黑安全会议，可关注俄乌冲突的相关进展与美俄乌三方会谈的进展。美国国内方面，
考虑到 2026 年是中期选举年，两党都不愿承担长期瘫痪政府的政治责任。预计在 1 月 31 日截止日前后，
双方可能达成某种形式的短期临时拨款法案，或者经历极短时间的“技术性关门”后迅速妥协。
非美发达市场维持平稳，边际分化。Bloomberg 和同花顺显示，受德国疲软的拖累，1 月欧元区制
造业 PMI 录得 49.4。
新兴市场 12 月景气度整体偏积极，小幅放缓。尽管越南、印度 12 月制造业 PMI 有所回落，但仍
明显位于荣枯线之上，增长核心动能充足。出口层面，越南和印度两国出口订单具备一定韧性，马来西亚、
泰国与菲律宾出口同比回落。

我们维持整个一季度国内宏观预期偏暖的判断，整体风险资产的投资环境仍然比较友好。我们建
议投资者在 2 月在国内宏观层面继续关注两条主线：第一，“十五五”开门红政策预期。第二，通胀
回升预期。

三、大类资产展望：关注结构性的进攻机会
（一）股指：政策暖风叠加通胀回升，仍推荐超配权益
展望 2 月，我们预计“十五五”规划开门红的政策预期以及通胀回升的趋势将成为权益市场的主
线宏观叙事，国内权益市场或继续震荡偏强，仍推荐超配 IC。如我们在本报告国内财政与货币政策展
望章节中所述，1 月财政、货币政策均有较多边际变化，这些政策端的变化均指向加力实现“十五五”
规划第一年的经济开门红。因此，我们预计财政政策和广义财政支出仍将靠前发力，而货币政策的边
际宽松变化时点或亦将偏向上半年甚至一季度。因此，我们认为一季度或是一个国内政策预期偏强的
窗口。此外，通胀中枢回升的预期也对权益市场有较强的利好，价格的改善有望提振企业盈利。在偏
暖政策环境，叠加企业盈利有望回升的宏观环境下，我们仍然推荐投资者在调整后继续超配股指。

观面地缘主导，委内瑞拉和伊朗局势是否升级仍然需要观察。基本面则看哈萨克斯坦供应恢复的进度
与极端天气的扰动；中期来看美页岩油产量调整存在滞后效应，产量中枢难出现实质性反转。油价在
地缘风险炒作情绪和供应扰动支撑下，下方空间受限。

（三）债券
2 月我们预计短端表现或仍好于中长端，整体赔率一般。逻辑仍主要在两点长期趋势上：①PPI
在 2026 年或将逐步回升，这意味着实际利率的趋势将总体下行，削弱了降息的必要性；②“反内卷”
政策的持续存在，也将推升价格回升预期，降低实际利率。此外，由于财政发力仍倾向于前置，短期
一季度债券供给预计也将偏强，也可能制约利率下行的空间。

CITIC Futures International Research 中信期货国际化研究
四、大类资产策略建议
2 月，我们更加强调市场降波过程中的防守，等待降波之后再度布局。国内权益市场的政策预期
和额外流动性依然可以提供上行的支撑。国债整体中性，短端机会好于长端，但赔率有限。贵金属中
的黄金维持多头标配，有色金属在宏观与供应逻辑支撑下趋势不改，但需警惕短期波动。黑色商品整
体偏震荡，原油或在地缘支撑下有所上行，但不确定性较大，因此建议保持观望。
2 月资产配置多空建议：
国内股指：多头超配中盘风格。关注：中证 500。
国债：整体中性，多头标配短端债券。关注：TS。
大宗商品：观望。有色金属关注短期波动风险，市场情绪可能进一步回调，关注降波后低吸做多
的机会。黑色、原油等商品或宽幅震荡，保持观望。
贵金属：多头标配，关注波动率趋稳和回调后的再度建仓机会。关注：沪金。
风险提示：1）地缘风险；2）美国经济意外走强；3）国内增量政策和经济修复不及预期；4）流动性
超预期收紧；5）美元指数超预期反弹。
